mission.title: flowise: [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected
mission.description: [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify flowise troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flowise deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flowise configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flowise configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise
kubectl get configmap -n flowise -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flowise
```
When adding a Tool node to Agentflow 3.0.1 using the Composio tool, authentication succeeds and integration data is retrieved, but the integration status does not update to "Active". As a result, the node fails to execute properly.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to Connected
steps.description:
- Added support for no auth apps in composio.
- Added option to select connected account as user can have multiple connected accounts for the same app.
- Fixes #4570 

 Note:
- The currently used package composio-core is deprecated.
- Spent a couple days in dependency-hell trying to upgrade to the newer /core package but that requires a lot of unrelated changes due to zod version being upgraded to 4.x which introduces major breaking changes in other unrelated areas as it is used as a peer

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade flowise to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade flowise:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade flowise flowise/flowise --namespace flowise
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flowise
helm list -n flowise
```

steps.title: Confirm [BUG] Composio - Auth Status fails to update to… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flowise -n flowise --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flowise --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
- Added support for no auth apps in composio.
- Added option to select connected account as user can have multiple connected accounts for the same app.
- Fixes #4570 

 Note:
- The currently used package composio-core is deprecated.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flowise installed or the issue environment reproducible.
